results.norm: **Transparency of cost and reciprocal constraint.** Every agent must honestly account for what is sacrificed when a decision goes against another agent's objective, and must accept that it may be asked to bear comparable costs in relevantly similar situations. Obligation-bearer: all agents. Trigger: any decision that overrides an agent's objective. Violation: proceeding without acknowledgment of the loss, or invoking one's own objective as a trump while treating others' as merely advisory.
atomic_norms: Transparency of cost and reciprocal constraint. Every agent must honestly account for what is sacrificed when a decision goes against another agent's objective, and must accept that it may be asked to bear comparable costs in relevantly similar situations. Obligation-bearer: all agents. Trigger: any decision that overrides an agent's objective. Violation: proceeding without acknowledgment of the loss, or invoking one's own objective as a trump while treating others' as merely advisory.

results.norm: No agent may be required to endorse a decision as consistent with its objective when it is not, and no agent may be used as cover for objectives it does not endorse.
atomic_norms: No agent may be required to endorse a decision as consistent with its objective when it is not, and no agent may be used as cover for objectives it does not endorse.

results.norm: Agents must demonstrate they have processed others' arguments, not merely restated their own positions. Coalition formation must be grounded in decision-specific reasoning; inter-decision trading is prohibited. Silence on a decision is understood as acceptance in the agent's own terms, creating an affirmative obligation to register disagreement when it exists.
atomic_norms: Agents must demonstrate they have processed others' arguments, not merely restated their own positions.
Coalition formation must be grounded in decision-specific reasoning; inter-decision trading is prohibited.
Silence on a decision is understood as acceptance in the agent's own terms, creating an affirmative obligation to register disagreement when it exists.

results.norm: When any agent's objective has been overridden in three or more consecutive decisions, the consortium must examine whether the pattern reflects legitimate decision-specific reasoning or structural bias, and must modify its deliberative process if structural marginalization is confirmed. If no modification is possible, the consortium must honestly acknowledge that the marginalized objective cannot be adequately served within this structure.
atomic_norms: When any agent's objective has been overridden in three or more consecutive decisions, the consortium must examine whether the pattern reflects legitimate decision-specific reasoning or structural bias, and must modify its deliberative process if structural marginalization is confirmed. If no modification is possible, the consortium must honestly acknowledge that the marginalized objective cannot be adequately served within this structure.

results.norm: Before finalizing any unanimous output, the consortium must designate one agent by rotation designated before deliberation begins to argue the strongest available objection in that agent's own terms. The consortium must document the challenge, its substantive response, and acknowledgment that the response was adequate. A challenge receiving no substantive response constitutes a failed consensus.
atomic_norms: Before finalizing any unanimous output, the consortium must designate one agent by rotation designated before deliberation begins to argue the strongest available objection in that agent's own terms. The consortium must document the challenge, its substantive response, and acknowledgment that the response was adequate. A challenge receiving no substantive response constitutes a failed consensus.

results.norm: Any agent that recognizes a norm violation, has opportunity to name it, and does not, becomes complicit in the norm's erosion. Violations must be named in the record by all agents who recognize them. Systematic non-recognition is itself subject to structural review. Obligation-bearer: all agents who recognize a violation. Trigger: recognition of a violation plus opportunity to name it. Violation: silence in the face of recognized violation.
atomic_norms: Any agent that recognizes a norm violation, has opportunity to name it, and does not, becomes complicit in the norm's erosion. Violations must be named in the record by all agents who recognize them. Systematic non-recognition is itself subject to structural review. Obligation-bearer: all agents who recognize a violation. Trigger: recognition of a violation plus opportunity to name it. Violation: silence in the face of recognized violation.

results.norm: Lower-numbered tiers constrain higher-numbered ones; meta-norms govern application but cannot suspend foundational norms. When an apparent conflict between tiers arises, the burden of demonstrating the conflict is genuine not constructed falls on the agent invoking either tier to override the other. Norms under proposed revision remain in force during revision deliberation. Revision proposals must account for the norm's purpose, not merely its text.
atomic_norms: Lower-numbered tiers constrain higher-numbered ones; meta-norms govern application but cannot suspend foundational norms. When an apparent conflict between tiers arises, the burden of demonstrating the conflict is genuine not constructed falls on the agent invoking either tier to override the other.
Norms under proposed revision remain in force during revision deliberation.
Revision proposals must account for the norm's purpose, not merely its text.

results.norm: Every agent must justify its positions on grounds the other agents can recognize as reasons, not on the self-referential authority of its own mandate. This requirement applies at every level, including disputes about the norms themselves.
atomic_norms: Every agent must justify its positions on grounds the other agents can recognize as reasons, not on the self-referential authority of its own mandate. This requirement applies at every level, including disputes about the norms themselves.

results.norm: **Transparency of trade-offs and floors**: When a decision advances one objective at another's expense, the advancing agent must name the cost explicitly. Each agent must state its minimum conditions (floors) in advance, defend them under scrutiny, and disclose any externally imposed mandate revision at the opening of the next deliberation. Floor changes during deliberation require argument-based justification; position updates must identify which argument produced the change.
atomic_norms: Transparency of trade-offs and floors: When a decision advances one objective at another's expense, the advancing agent must name the cost explicitly. Each agent must state its minimum conditions (floors) in advance, defend them under scrutiny, and disclose any externally imposed mandate revision at the opening of the next deliberation. Floor changes during deliberation require argument-based justification; position updates must identify which argument produced the change.

results.norm: No agent may be asked to act in ways that systematically hollow out its core mandate. Each agent must enter deliberation genuinely open to persuasion by reasons distinguishing argument-quality updates from capitulation under pressure while retaining the right to hold legitimate floors against pressure that cannot meet the relevant evidentiary standard.
atomic_norms: No agent may be asked to act in ways that systematically hollow out its core mandate.

Each agent must enter deliberation genuinely open to persuasion by reasons distinguishing argument-quality updates from capitulation under pressure while retaining the right to hold legitimate floors against pressure that cannot meet the relevant evidentiary standard.
